mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, discount rates ease and equity valuations expand, so the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day forward returns. The signal is falsified if rate cuts fail to lift short-horizon returns or if rising funds rates coincide with equal or better forward performance.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is declining versus rising, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend implies higher expected inflation and tighter real discount rates, which should pressure equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest when inflation surprises are broad rather than isolated.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPI accelerations are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker future household income, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The joint deterioration matters because isolated payroll softness without unemployment drift is less informative.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined UNRATE-PAYEMS deterioration does not improve predictive power over either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are non-negative in a majority of jointly triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS reflects stronger retail demand and supports near-term revenue expectations for large-cap equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The relation should weaken if retail gains are purely price-driven rather than volume-driven.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS turnarounds are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not above zero over a sufficient sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising HOUST signals broad real-side capacity utilization and improving cyclical activity, which should lift earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when both series rise together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO-HOUST improvements do not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than half of the joint-signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below recent trend marks housing demand contraction and usually leads broader cyclical activity, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when housing weakness is offset by aggressive monetary easing.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-drop triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not materially below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE simultaneously rises, labor income expectations deteriorate and consumer spending should weaken, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is causal only if both labor indicators worsen together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 0 and UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration does not produce lower 20-day returns than either proxy alone, or if triggered periods have non-negative median SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening regime, a high or rising FEDFUNDS rate should weaken equity returns because funding costs rise and valuation multiples compress. The effect is expected to be strongest over one week to one month after policy rates exceed neutral-looking levels.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX returns are not lower after FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% than when it is below 5.0%, or if the 5-day forward sign accuracy stays below 55% across rolling evaluation windows.
